helped with that. This is really what we're talking about here. Specifically in this program, there are so many reasons why you should use the newsletter. But for this bonus CD, how does the newsletter help with referrals? Well, the first way it helps is that they actually remember who you are. So a lot of studies have been done that the peak moment for referrals is within 72 hours of a positive interaction with your company. So that means that if you see your clients once every quarter, once every year, once every six months, whatever it is, that basically the time that you're most likely to get a referral from them is within the first three days after they've done business. And that's kind of the maximum period. Some folks will forget about you a lot sooner. Some might remember you a little bit longer. But that's the period. And so you cannot get a referral if they don't remember who you are. They don't remember the product or service you provide. You know, if they're not thinking about you, it's just impossible to get a referral. So you've got to stay top of mind with them. That's one thing. Two, you need to be able to promote the referral, whatever program it is, whatever that happens to be that you're running. If you happen to be running a cool referral contest, I know we have a new one we just launched. And you've got to promote that. You've got to get that out there. And the newsletter is a great way to talk about that. You need to build relationships. The newsletter builds a relationship if done correctly. Now there's a lot of ways to do a newsletter wrong, just like using any media. There's a lot of incorrect ways to use a media. But if you do it correctly, you will start to build a relationship. People will know and like you. And then when they know and like you, they're much more inclined to refer more business over to you. It is no longer good enough to just provide a good product or service. Everyone expects that from you already. That is now the minimum level that they expect from you. So you really need to provide them with top notch quality, but you also need to go above and beyond and build those relationships. Help them. You know, Zig Ziglar said, if you help enough people, get what they want, you'll get what you want. And you can actually do that via newsletters. Help your clients get more of what they want, and in turn, you'll get more of what you want. So I can explain that more. Yeah, so what's really cool here is also the content of the newsletter, and we're going to talk about in detail the content, but the content of the newsletter and the way that you instruct people and the way that you set it up for people and the way that you teach people to do it, is unique and will actually get people talking about it. So if somebody is out with their golfing buddies, right, and they get the newsletter from their dentist, and you just keep using the dentist's rest as an example, everybody works at any restaurant, they'll say, hey, I can tell you something cool that was in this newsletter that my dentist sent me, right? So let's talk about the actual content. And first of all, I want to just go over overall content, and then, Sean, what I'd like to do is really dissect the newsletter and what literally should be on each page of the newsletter from the front cover to the back cover. But first of all, let me ask you about content. What kind of content works best? So if I'm a restaurant owner, should I, or a butcher, a baker, a candlestick maker, should I be have just stuff about my business by auto repair store? Should the whole newsletter be about auto repair? Yeah, okay, so that's a mistake, and we can talk about mistakes if you want as well, but that's a huge mistake we see that happens too often, is that that's what they talk about. You're an auto mechanic, and you talk about just the repairing cars, and I've got to be honest with you, man, I don't want to know how to change my own oil. That's why you're going there! Yeah, absolutely, and I don't want to know what's required to do it. I want to give you my $25, and I want you to take care of it for me. And if you send me a newsletter about how to change my oil, I'd rather you pull my toenail out. I don't want to read that. I don't have time to read that. So really what you want to do with content, there are at least four types of content that you want to have. Obviously it can sometimes be a little bit specific to each business, but you want to have personal content. Now, let me talk about this, because this is where people get hung up on the personal content, and they say stuff like, oh, no one cares what's going on in my life, why would I want to tell them about my vacation, or my kids, or whatever the case may be. I always find that to be funny, because there's this kind of new company, although it's really big, it's called Facebook, and all it is about is what's going on in people's personal lives, and it's super popular, and you may have heard of it even, Dave. I like to call it the Facebook.
